STATION SIX: FILTRATE PATH AND BLOOD FLOW THROUGH KIDNEY As a group, determine the route of urine through the various ducts of the kidney, originating at the glomerulus and ending with the urethra. Be sure to identify where along that path each of the structures on the vocabulary list is located. As a group, determine the course of blood through the vessels of the kidney. Note: The following three pages are left blank for the purpose of drawing out these two pathways. 144 MALGOSIA WILK-BLASZCZAK
